item.dc:creator: Lawrence Hurley
item.description: Delivering another major blow to LGBTQ rights, the Supreme Court on Tuesday upheld state laws that ban transgender athletes from participating in girls' and women's sports.

media:content.media:text: Becky Pepper-Jackson outside the Supreme Court
media:content.media:description: Becky Pepper-Jackson, 16, challenged West Virginia's law banning transgender athletes from girls' sports.

item.title: Supreme Court upholds bans on transgender athletes in girls’ and women’s sports

item.dc:creator: Jonathan Allen
Katherine Doyle
item.description: The Supreme Court and the Republican-controlled Senate have erected guardrails that limit President Donald Trump’s ability to make it harder for citizens to vote, and he’s not happy about that.

media:content.media:text: US President Donald Trump waves as he boards Air Force One before departing from Reading Regional Airport in Berks County, Pa. on June 23, 2026.
media:content.media:description: US President Donald Trump waves as he boards Air Force One before departing from Reading Regional Airport in Berks County, Pa. on June 23, 2026.

item.title: Trump’s voting restrictions face roadblocks from Congress and the Supreme Court

item.dc:creator: Jonathan Allen
Katherine Doyle
item.description: The Supreme Court and the Republican-controlled Senate have erected guardrails that limit President Donald Trump’s ability to make it harder for citizens to vote, and he’s not happy about that.

media:content.media:text: US President Donald Trump waves as he boards Air Force One before departing from Reading Regional Airport in Berks County, Pa. on June 23, 2026.
media:content.media:description: US President Donald Trump waves as he boards Air Force One before departing from Reading Regional Airport in Berks County, Pa. on June 23, 2026.

item.title: Trump’s voting restrictions face roadblocks from Congress and the Supreme Court

item.description: El gol que le hubiera dado la victoria a los alemanes gracias al cabezazo de Tah es anulado por una falta de Anton sobre el portero paraguayo Gill.

media:content.media:title: Gol anulado a Alemania en tiempos extra por obstrucción al portero
media:content.media:description: El gol que le hubiera dado la victoria a los alemanes gracias al cabezazo de Tah es anulado por una falta de Anton sobre el portero paraguayo Gill.

item.title: Gol anulado a Alemania en tiempos extra por obstrucción al portero

item.description: En una intensa definición por penales, los "Leones del Atlas" se impusieron 3-2; luego de los cobros fallados por Kluivert, Timber y Summerville de los Países Bajos, Ismael Saibari firmó el cobro decisivo que selló el pase a 8vos en Monterrey.

media:content.media:title: ¡Tensión pura! Revive la tanda de penales con la que Marruecos eliminó a Países Bajos en el Mundial
media:content.media:description: En una intensa definición por penales, los "Leones del Atlas" se impusieron 3-2; luego de los cobros fallados por Kluivert, Timber y Summerville de los Países Bajos, Ismael Saibari firmó el cobro decisivo que selló el pase a 8vos en Monterrey.

item.title: ¡Tensión pura! Revive la tanda de penales con la que Marruecos eliminó a Países Bajos en el Mundial

item.description: Brasil remontó para eliminar a Japón, Paraguay dio el gran golpe al dejar fuera a Alemania en los penales y Marruecos eliminó a Países Bajos desde los once pasos en otra jornada vibrante de los dieciseisavos de final.

media:content.media:title: Paraguay y Marruecos sorprenden, Brasil responde y el Mundial pierde a dos gigantes
media:content.media:description: Brasil remontó para eliminar a Japón, Paraguay dio el gran golpe al dejar fuera a Alemania en los penales y Marruecos eliminó a Países Bajos desde los once pasos en otra jornada vibrante de los dieciseisavos de final.

item.title: Paraguay y Marruecos sorprenden, Brasil responde y el Mundial pierde a dos gigantes

item.description: Paraguay dio la sorpresa al eliminar a Alemania, Brasil selló su pase con un gol en los minutos finales y Marruecos dejó fuera a Países Bajos tras una dramática tanda de penales.

media:content.media:title: Paraguay elimina a Alemania, Brasil avanza sobre la hora y Marruecos despide a Países Bajos
media:content.media:description: Paraguay dio la sorpresa al eliminar a Alemania, Brasil selló su pase con un gol en los minutos finales y Marruecos dejó fuera a Países Bajos tras una dramática tanda de penales.

item.title: Paraguay elimina a Alemania, Brasil avanza sobre la hora y Marruecos despide a Países Bajos

item.description: La afición paraguaya festejó con todo la histórica clasificación a los octavos de final. En Boston, los brasileños vivieron con intensidad el duelo ante Japón, mientras las aficiones de Marruecos y Países Bajos estan listos para su partido en Monterrey.

media:content.media:title: Paraguay celebra su pase a cuartos y Marruecos junto a Países Bajos encienden México
media:content.media:description: La afición paraguaya festejó con todo la histórica clasificación a los octavos de final. En Boston, los brasileños vivieron con intensidad el duelo ante Japón, mientras las aficiones de Marruecos y Países Bajos estan listos para su partido en Monterrey.

item.title: Paraguay celebra su pase a cuartos y Marruecos junto a Países Bajos encienden México

item.description: Achraf Hakimi agradeció el apoyo de la afición mexicana tras la histórica clasificación de Marruecos ante Países Bajos. El defensor destacó la fortaleza mental del equipo y aseguró sentirse muy orgulloso por el pase a los octavos de final.

media:content.media:title: Hakimi: “Quiero agradecer a México por todo el apoyo”
media:content.media:description: Achraf Hakimi agradeció el apoyo de la afición mexicana tras la histórica clasificación de Marruecos ante Países Bajos. El defensor destacó la fortaleza mental del equipo y aseguró sentirse muy orgulloso por el pase a los octavos de final.

item.title: Hakimi: “Quiero agradecer a México por todo el apoyo”

item.description: Virgil van Dijk analizó la eliminación de Países Bajos ante Marruecos en los dieciseisavos de final de la Copa Mundial de la FIFA 2026. El defensor lamentó cómo el equipo perdió el control del partido en la tanda de penales.

media:content.media:title: Virgil van Dijk: "Al final, en el tiempo añadido te echan atrás"
media:content.media:description: Virgil van Dijk analizó la eliminación de Países Bajos ante Marruecos en los dieciseisavos de final de la Copa Mundial de la FIFA 2026. El defensor lamentó cómo el equipo perdió el control del partido en la tanda de penales.

item.title: Virgil van Dijk: "Al final, en el tiempo añadido te echan atrás"

item.description: Issa Diop aseguró que Marruecos nunca dejó de creer y destacó la unión del equipo tras eliminar a Países Bajos en la tanda de penales. El defensor, autor del gol del empate, dedicó la clasificación al pueblo marroquí.

media:content.media:title: Issa Diop: “Este equipo demostró que nunca se rinde”
media:content.media:description: Issa Diop aseguró que Marruecos nunca dejó de creer y destacó la unión del equipo tras eliminar a Países Bajos en la tanda de penales. El defensor, autor del gol del empate, dedicó la clasificación al pueblo marroquí.

item.title: Issa Diop: “Este equipo demostró que nunca se rinde”

item.description: El mediocampista cobró el último disparo de la tanda y con un derechazo cruzado venció al arquero neerlandés para sellar el 3-2 y el pase de los "Leones del Atlas" a los 8vos, donde enfrentarán a Canadá. La "Naranja Mecánica" volvió a decir adiós.

media:content.media:title: ¡Épico! Así fue el penal decisivo de Saibari con el que Marruecos eliminó a Países Bajos del Mundial
media:content.media:description: El mediocampista cobró el último disparo de la tanda y con un derechazo cruzado venció al arquero neerlandés para sellar el 3-2 y el pase de los "Leones del Atlas" a los 8vos, donde enfrentarán a Canadá. La "Naranja Mecánica" volvió a decir adiós.

item.title: ¡Épico! Así fue el penal decisivo de Saibari con el que Marruecos eliminó a Países Bajos del Mundial

item.description: Tras empatar en tiempo regular con gol de último minuto, los "Leones del Atlas" se impusieron en la tanda de penales por 3-2 y firmaron la segunda sorpresa del Mundial en esta fase; sellaron su pase a los 8vos y se enfrentarán a uno de los anfitriones.

media:content.media:title: ¡Marruecos avanza! Vence en penales a Países Bajos y se cita con Canadá en los 8avos del Mundial
media:content.media:description: Tras empatar en tiempo regular con gol de último minuto, los "Leones del Atlas" se impusieron en la tanda de penales por 3-2 y firmaron la segunda sorpresa del Mundial en esta fase; sellaron su pase a los 8vos y se enfrentarán a uno de los anfitriones.

item.title: ¡Marruecos avanza! Vence en penales a Países Bajos y se cita con Canadá en los 8avos del Mundial

item.description: Nuestros expertos analizan la victoria de Brasil sobre Japón en los dieciseisavos de final y la sorpresiva eliminación de Alemania a manos de Paraguay, uno de los resultados más destacados de la jornada mundialista.

media:content.media:title: ¿Brasil confirma su candidatura y Paraguay firma la gran sorpresa? Nuestros expertos lo analizan
media:content.media:description: Nuestros expertos analizan la victoria de Brasil sobre Japón en los dieciseisavos de final y la sorpresiva eliminación de Alemania a manos de Paraguay, uno de los resultados más destacados de la jornada mundialista.

item.title: ¿Brasil confirma su candidatura y Paraguay firma la gran sorpresa? Nuestros expertos lo analizan

item.description: President Donald Trump is sending a high-level team to the Middle East to resume peace negotiations with Iran after days of attacks but both sides appear far from any formal agreement. NBC’s Keir Simmons reports for TODAY.

media:content.media:title: US Seeks Peace Talks, but Iran Says a Deal Is Far Off
media:content.media:description: President Donald Trump is sending a high-level team to the Middle East to resume peace negotiations with Iran after days of attacks but both sides appear far from any formal agreement. NBC’s Keir Simmons reports for TODAY.

item.title: US Seeks Peace Talks, but Iran Says a Deal Is Far Off

item.dc:creator: Freddie Clayton
Chantal Da Silva
item.description: The U.N. report alleged Israel has a “deliberate strategy” of targeting children in the occupied West Bank and Gaza Strip, which Israel rejects.

media:content.media:text: Three year old Rayan Abu al-Ajeen killed by Israeli forces.
media:content.media:description: 3-year-old Rayan Abu al-Ajeen.

media:content.media:text: Palestinians protest confiscation of land in occupied West Bank
media:content.media:description: 3-year-old Rayan Abu al-Ajeen.

media:content.media:text: Palestinian children in the West Bank
media:content.media:description: 3-year-old Rayan Abu al-Ajeen.

media:content.media:text: Residents continued to live in the area near the Israeli military's "yellow line" amid ongoing operations and widespread damage.
media:content.media:description: 3-year-old Rayan Abu al-Ajeen.

item.title: 3-year-old shot dead in Gaza as U.N. report accuses Israel of targeting children

item.dc:creator: Mike Hixenbaugh
item.description: For a half-century, U.S. policy has pushed to keep disabled Americans out of institutions. Now Rebecca Anger and others fear that era could end.

media:content.media:text: A split composite image of Rebecca Anger having her legs and feet fastened into a wheelchair, and a plant by a windowsill
media:content.media:description: Rebecca Anger, a 41-year-old lawyer in Chicago, relies on caregivers to get through each workday.

media:content.media:text: Christine O'Conner helps fasten Rebecca Anger's feet into her wheelchair as she's seated
media:content.media:description: Rebecca Anger, a 41-year-old lawyer in Chicago, relies on caregivers to get through each workday.

media:content.media:text: Christine O'Connor, a preschool teacher, cares for Rebecca about six hours each week under a Medicaid program that pays $19.50 an hour.
media:content.media:description: Rebecca Anger, a 41-year-old lawyer in Chicago, relies on caregivers to get through each workday.

media:content.media:text: Greg Anger, left, and Rebecca Anger sit outside in their wheelchairs
media:content.media:description: Rebecca Anger, a 41-year-old lawyer in Chicago, relies on caregivers to get through each workday.

media:content.media:text: As seen through a doorway, a woman assists Rebecca Anger who is seated in a wheelchair, as Greg Anger watches, also seated in a wheelchair
media:content.media:description: Rebecca Anger, a 41-year-old lawyer in Chicago, relies on caregivers to get through each workday.

media:content.media:credit: Courtesy Cindy Stevens; Courtesy LaTisha Brown
media:content.media:text:
A split composite image, on left: A portrait of Evan Stevens smiling against a blue backdrop
On right: LaTisha Brown smiles while wearing a white dress and holding a bouquet of flowers, she is seated on a wheelchair out of frame
media:content.media:description: Rebecca Anger, a 41-year-old lawyer in Chicago, relies on caregivers to get through each workday.

media:content.media:text: Two photos of Rebecca Anger's childhood are slightly overlaid atop on another on a white background
media:content.media:description: Rebecca Anger, a 41-year-old lawyer in Chicago, relies on caregivers to get through each workday.

media:content.media:text: Rebecca Anger as a child, smiling, seated on a wheelchair with a floral design backpack on her lap
media:content.media:description: Rebecca Anger, a 41-year-old lawyer in Chicago, relies on caregivers to get through each workday.

media:content.media:text: A split composite image, on the left, Rebecca's hand is on a wheelchair controller, on the right, Rebecca is seen on a street mirror in her wheelchair traveling on the sidewalk
media:content.media:description: Rebecca Anger, a 41-year-old lawyer in Chicago, relies on caregivers to get through each workday.

media:content.media:text: Rebecca Anger, center, is seated in a wheelchair while surrounded by three of her siblings, posing for a portrait outside
media:content.media:description: Rebecca Anger, a 41-year-old lawyer in Chicago, relies on caregivers to get through each workday.

media:content.media:text: Greg, left, and Rebecca Anger are seated in wheelchairs while Greg is in a gray wedding tuxedo and Rebecca wears a white wedding dress and holds a bouquet of flowers
media:content.media:description: Rebecca Anger, a 41-year-old lawyer in Chicago, relies on caregivers to get through each workday.

media:content.media:text: A split composite image: photo on the left shows a framed picture of Ruth Bader Ginsburg wearing a baseball cap next to a label that reads "Rebecca Anger, Staff Attorney", in the middle photo - assistive devices, two prongs attached to long handles gathered together, and photo on the right shows Rebecca using the devices, controlled by her mouth, to do work on her laptop
media:content.media:description: Rebecca Anger, a 41-year-old lawyer in Chicago, relies on caregivers to get through each workday.

media:content.media:text: Rebecca Anger is seen riding the bus in the priority section while seated in a wheelchair
media:content.media:description: Rebecca Anger, a 41-year-old lawyer in Chicago, relies on caregivers to get through each workday.

media:content.media:text: A split composite image of a small leafy plant on a window sill, left, and Rebecca Anger close up profile portrait
media:content.media:description: Rebecca Anger, a 41-year-old lawyer in Chicago, relies on caregivers to get through each workday.

item.title: Paralyzed at 7, she fought for the life she wanted. Medicaid cuts threaten to take it away.

item.description: Officials in Venezuela say the death toll from the back-to-back earthquakes last week has risen to more than 1,700 people. It comes as rescue teams from around the world continue to comb through the rubble of toppled building for survivors. The United Nations estimates the death toll could climb as high as 10,000 people.

media:content.media:title: Venezuelan Earthquakes: Window for Finding Survivors Shrinks
media:content.media:description: Officials in Venezuela say the death toll from the back-to-back earthquakes last week has risen to more than 1,700 people. It comes as rescue teams from around the world continue to comb through the rubble of toppled building for survivors. The United Nations estimates the death toll could climb as high as 10,000 people.

item.title: Venezuelan Earthquakes: Window for Finding Survivors Shrinks

item.dc:creator: Ben Kamisar
Annelise Hanson
Adam Edelman
Bridget Bowman
item.description: Veteran Colorado Democrats running for Senate, governor and House are facing another round of insurgent challengers Tuesday.

media:content.media:text: Democratic House Reps. Rep. Diana DeGette, D-CO.
media:content.media:description: Rep. Diana DeGette, D-Colo., faces a primary challenge from the left Tuesday.

media:content.media:text: Melat Kiros
media:content.media:description: Rep. Diana DeGette, D-Colo., faces a primary challenge from the left Tuesday.

item.title: Colorado’s primaries present the next test for the Democratic establishment

item.dc:creator: Lawrence Hurley
item.description: Delivering another major blow to LGBTQ rights, the Supreme Court on Tuesday upheld state laws that ban transgender athletes from participating in girls' and women's sports.

media:content.media:text: Becky Pepper-Jackson outside the Supreme Court
media:content.media:description: Becky Pepper-Jackson, 16, challenged West Virginia's law banning transgender athletes from girls' sports.

item.title: Supreme Court upholds bans on transgender athletes in girls’ and women’s sports

item.dc:creator: Daniel Arkin
Steve Kopack
item.description: The British government suggested Tuesday it may challenge Paramount Skydance’s blockbuster $110 billion takeover of Warner Bros.

media:content.media:text: The Paramount office building in Los Angeles.
media:content.media:description: The Paramount office building in Los Angeles.

item.title: British government may ‘intervene’ in Paramount’s takeover of Warner Bros.

item.dc:creator: Amina Kilpatrick
item.description: Serena Williams is returning to the singles court Tuesday, following her move away from tennis in 2022.

media:content.media:text: Serena Williams
media:content.media:description: Serena Williams during a practice session on day three of the HSBC Championships at The Queen's Club in London.

item.title: Serena Williams returns to Wimbledon after 2022 retirement

item.dc:creator: Elmira Aliieva
item.description: More than 60 drones targeted Moscow overnight, the city’s mayor said Tuesday, marking another major aerial attack on the Russian capital as Ukraine intensifies its long-range weapons campaign.

media:content.media:credit: Governor of Moscow region Andrei Vorobyov
media:content.media:text: Aftermath of a Ukrainian drone attack in Moscow region
media:content.media:description: Images released by the Moscow region's governor, Gov. Andrei Vorobyov, on Tuesday appear to show damage to a home in the town of Yegoryevsk, following a Ukrainian drone attack overnight.

media:content.media:text: Aftermath of a Ukrainian drone attack in Moscow region
media:content.media:description: Images released by the Moscow region's governor, Gov. Andrei Vorobyov, on Tuesday appear to show damage to a home in the town of Yegoryevsk, following a Ukrainian drone attack overnight.

media:content.media:text: TOPSHOT-UKRAINE-RUSSIA-CONFLICT-WAR
media:content.media:description: Images released by the Moscow region's governor, Gov. Andrei Vorobyov, on Tuesday appear to show damage to a home in the town of Yegoryevsk, following a Ukrainian drone attack overnight.

media:content.media:text: Image: Vladimir Putin on stage addressing an audience
media:content.media:description: Images released by the Moscow region's governor, Gov. Andrei Vorobyov, on Tuesday appear to show damage to a home in the town of Yegoryevsk, following a Ukrainian drone attack overnight.

item.title: Russian satellite communications center hit, Ukraine says, as drones target Moscow

item.dc:creator: Chantal Da Silva
item.description: Rescuers crowd around a newborn wearing only a diaper and bundled in a striped blanket in a video that offers a glimmer of hope to families whose loved ones are still missing after Venezuela’s huge earthquakes last week.

media:content.media:text: Rescuers pull a baby from the rubble of a collapsed building following deadly earthquakes that struck Venezuela on June 24, in La Guaira
media:content.media:description: Mother and 18-day-old baby rescued from rubble of building 32 hours after Venezuela earthquakes

media:content.media:text: Rescuers pull a baby from the rubble of a collapsed building following deadly earthquakes that struck Venezuela on June 24, in La Guaira
media:content.media:description: Baby Juan David after he was rescued, left, and his mother, Dayana Patino, trapped under the rubble, right.

media:content.media:text: The mother and baby receiving medical treatment following the rescue.
media:content.media:description: The mother and baby receiving medical treatment following the rescue.

media:content.media:text: TOPSHOT-VENEZUELA-EARTHQUAKE-AFTERMATH
media:content.media:description: Buildings destroyed following twin earthquakes in Caraballeda, La Guaira state, Venezuela, on Monday.

media:content.media:credit: Department of State & VA-TF1; USA-01 - Urban Search and Rescue
media:content.media:text: Rescue teams pull a 9-month old from the rubble in Venezuela, in a video released Saturday.
media:content.media:description: Rescue teams pull a 9-month old from the rubble in Venezuela, in a video released Saturday.

item.title: Video of a newborn and his mother rescued after days under rubble offers hope in Venezuela

item.dc:creator: Mike Hixenbaugh
item.description: For a half-century, U.S. policy has pushed to keep disabled Americans out of institutions. Now Rebecca Anger and others fear that era could end.

media:content.media:text: A split composite image of Rebecca Anger having her legs and feet fastened into a wheelchair, and a plant by a windowsill
media:content.media:description: Rebecca Anger, a 41-year-old lawyer in Chicago, relies on caregivers to get through each workday.

media:content.media:text: Christine O'Conner helps fasten Rebecca Anger's feet into her wheelchair as she's seated
media:content.media:description: Rebecca Anger, a 41-year-old lawyer in Chicago, relies on caregivers to get through each workday.

media:content.media:text: Christine O'Connor, a preschool teacher, cares for Rebecca about six hours each week under a Medicaid program that pays $19.50 an hour.
media:content.media:description: Rebecca Anger, a 41-year-old lawyer in Chicago, relies on caregivers to get through each workday.

media:content.media:text: Greg Anger, left, and Rebecca Anger sit outside in their wheelchairs
media:content.media:description: Rebecca Anger, a 41-year-old lawyer in Chicago, relies on caregivers to get through each workday.

media:content.media:text: As seen through a doorway, a woman assists Rebecca Anger who is seated in a wheelchair, as Greg Anger watches, also seated in a wheelchair
media:content.media:description: Rebecca Anger, a 41-year-old lawyer in Chicago, relies on caregivers to get through each workday.

media:content.media:credit: Courtesy Cindy Stevens; Courtesy LaTisha Brown

A split composite image, on left: A portrait of Evan Stevens smiling against a blue backdrop
On right: LaTisha Brown smiles while wearing a white dress and holding a bouquet of flowers, she is seated on a wheelchair out of frame
media:content.media:description: Rebecca Anger, a 41-year-old lawyer in Chicago, relies on caregivers to get through each workday.

media:content.media:text: Two photos of Rebecca Anger's childhood are slightly overlaid atop on another on a white background

media:content.media:text: Rebecca Anger as a child, smiling, seated on a wheelchair with a floral design backpack on her lap

media:content.media:description: Rebecca Anger, a 41-year-old lawyer in Chicago, relies on caregivers to get through each workday.

media:content.media:text: Rebecca Anger, center, is seated in a wheelchair while surrounded by three of her siblings, posing for a portrait outside
media:content.media:description: Rebecca Anger, a 41-year-old lawyer in Chicago, relies on caregivers to get through each workday.

media:content.media:description: Rebecca Anger, a 41-year-old lawyer in Chicago, relies on caregivers to get through each workday.

media:content.media:text: Rebecca Anger is seen riding the bus in the priority section while seated in a wheelchair
media:content.media:description: Rebecca Anger, a 41-year-old lawyer in Chicago, relies on caregivers to get through each workday.

media:content.media:text: A split composite image of a small leafy plant on a window sill, left, and Rebecca Anger close up profile portrait
media:content.media:description: Rebecca Anger, a 41-year-old lawyer in Chicago, relies on caregivers to get through each workday.

item.title: Paralyzed at 7, she fought for the life she wanted. Medicaid cuts threaten to take it away.
